text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy village of Whiskerbinks, there lived a girl named Olive. She loved butterflies more than anything else. Whenever she saw one fluttering around her garden border, she'd sit down and watch it for hours. One particular bright orange butterfly made her heart skip beats.

One lazy summer day, Olive decided to go bird watching near Maple Hill. She brought a bunch of shiny jars stuffed full of flowers. She hoped to catch some curious birds.

After walking for a while, she spots a figure perched high in a big oak tree. It was an old lady owl. She told Olive stories about old times. Then Owl said her granddaughter sometimes sat there just like Olive. Owl searched higher. There! There was Ladybird!

They became friends soon enough. Ladybird told Olive about her adventures. She'd chase beetles nocturnously!

One moonlit night Owl told Olive tales of moth wars. They talked stars until dawn.

One day Ladybird said goodbye
